sections.context: Agents and humans regularly guess `--label` on `sd create` because `sd list`, `sd ready`, and `sd search` already accept `--label` for filtering. Today commander rejects the unknown option, forcing a retry. This is a low-risk, agent-friendly UX fix: add `--label` as a hidden alias for `--labels` on `sd create` so both forms work without changing the canonical flag or documentation surface.
sections.reproduction:
Steps to reproduce on main:
1. `cd` into a seeds-initialized project (or run `sd init` in a tmp dir).
2. Run `sd create --title "x" --label "foo,bar"`.
Observed: commander exits non-zero with `error: unknown option '--label' (Did you mean --labels?)` and no issue is created.
Expected: the issue is created with labels `["foo", "bar"]`, identical behavior to passing `--labels "foo,bar"`.
sections.root_cause: src/commands/create.ts only declares `--labels <labels>` on the commander subcommand (line 128) and only reads `flags.labels` in `run()` (line 67). There is no `--label` alias, so commander short-circuits before the action runs. The fix is purely additive: declare a hidden `--label` alias on the commander surface and let `run()` fall back to `flags.label` when `flags.labels` is absent. No schema, storage, or formatter changes are required — labels are already comma-split, lowercased, and dedup-filtered downstream.
sections.approach: Two-file change, no public API break: (1) In `src/commands/create.ts`, register a hidden `--label <labels>` option using commander's `Option(...).hideHelp(true)` so `--help` output stays clean, and forward `opts.label` into the internal arg vector as `--labels <value>` (preferring `opts.labels` when both are passed, matching commander's last-wins semantics for the canonical flag). Also accept `flags.label` as a fallback in the in-process `run()` path so the test harness (which calls `run(args)` directly via the parseArgs helper) sees the alias. (2) Add a focused test in `src/commands/create.test.ts` covering both the commander entry point and the alias-only case. (3) Note the alias under `## [Unreleased]` in `CHANGELOG.md`. Rejected alternatives: renaming `--labels` to `--label` (breaking change for existing scripts); leaving the alias visible in `--help` (clutters the canonical surface — bug explicitly asks for hidden).

steps.title: Add hidden --label alias to sd create (commander + run parser)
steps.description:
Edit `src/commands/create.ts`:

1. Import `Option` from commander alongside the existing `Command` type import.
2. In `register(program)`, replace the `.option("--labels <labels>", "Comma-separated labels")` line with two registrations: the canonical visible `--labels` plus `program.command(...).addOption(new Option("--label <labels>", "Alias for --labels").hideHelp())`. Concretely: keep the existing `.option("--labels <labels>", ...)` and chain `.addOption(new Option("--label <labels>", "Alias for --labels").hideHelp())` before `.option("--json", ...)`.
3. Extend the action's destructured opts type with `label?: string;` and, in the args-building block, push `"--labels", opts.label` when `opts.labels` is unset and `opts.label` is set (prefer the canonical flag if both are supplied).
4. In `run()`, change the `labelsRaw` derivation (current line 67) from `typeof flags.labels === "string" ? flags.labels : undefined` to also fall back to `flags.label` when `flags.labels` is absent — e.g. `const labelsRaw = typeof flags.labels === "string" ? flags.labels : typeof flags.label === "string" ? flags.label : undefined;`.

Do not change README, do not change the canonical `--labels` flag, do not alter the labels normalization pipeline (still comma-split, lowercased, deduped). Keep biome/tsc clean (`noUncheckedIndexedAccess`).

steps.title: Add test coverage for --label alias on sd create
steps.description:
Edit `src/commands/create.test.ts` to add a test case (use the existing in-process `runCli`/`runCliJson` harness, matching the style of neighboring tests) that:

1. Runs `sd create --title "alias check" --label "foo,bar" --json` against a fresh tmp seeds dir.
2. Asserts exit code 0 and that the created issue (looked up via the returned id) has `labels: ["foo", "bar"]` — i.e. byte-identical to the `--labels` path.
3. Optionally add a second assertion: when both `--labels "a"` and `--label "b"` are passed, `--labels` wins (so this captures the precedence rule from step 1).

Do not delete or rewrite existing `--labels` tests; this is additive. Follow the colocated real-I/O convention (mx-d82ab9): use `mkdtemp` via the existing test helpers, no mocks.

steps.title: Note --label alias under Unreleased in CHANGELOG.md
steps.description: Edit `CHANGELOG.md`: under the existing `## [Unreleased]` heading (line 8), add a bullet under a `### Added` (or matching) subsection: `- `sd create` now accepts `--label` as a hidden alias for `--labels` (seeds-76cf).` Follow Keep a Changelog conventions (mx-b3a697). Do not touch any other version section, do not add link refs at the bottom (the existing Unreleased link suffices).

steps.title: Release: run /release per .claude/commands/release.md
steps.description: Run the project's `/release` workflow as defined in `.claude/commands/release.md`. This step is blocked by all preceding steps; it should only run once the alias is implemented, tested, and changelogged.

acceptance: `sd create --title "x" --label "foo,bar"` exits 0 and produces an issue with labels ["foo","bar"], identical to the --labels form.
`sd create --help` does NOT list `--label` (alias is hidden via Option.hideHelp()).
Both --labels and --label paths share the same comma-split / lowercase / dedup normalization.
When --labels and --label are both supplied, --labels wins.
`bun test src/commands/create.test.ts` passes including the new alias test.
`bun run lint` and `bun run typecheck` are clean.
CHANGELOG.md has an Unreleased entry referencing seeds-76cf.

sections.context: Code-quality patrol surfaced six small inconsistencies across the CLI: identical helper duplicated in three list-style commands, blocked output missing plan parity, drift risk between version.ts and package.json in the upgrade command, palette/style inconsistencies in upgrade output, unknown-command error skipping --json mode in the top-level dispatch, and the init gitattributes patch not checking each merge=union line independently.
sections.behavior_invariant: All existing CLI surfaces keep their current observable contracts: every command still accepts the same flags and exits with the same codes for the same inputs; --json payloads remain a strict superset (new keys may appear, but no existing key is renamed, removed, or retyped); human output keeps the forest palette (brand ✓, accent, muted) and printWarning/printSuccess/printError helpers; sd init keeps merge=union for all three JSONL files; sd doctor's check count is unchanged; bun test, bun run lint, and bun run typecheck all stay green.
sections.approach: Each finding lands as an isolated, single-file (or single-helper-extraction) PR. No public API changes, no new commands, no dependency edits. Where helpers exist (plan-context, output.ts, version.ts), prefer reusing them over inlining or re-reading sources. Tests are updated only where the observable behavior is touched (e.g. blocked --json now carries plan_status/plan_children when present; init re-runs add missing merge=union lines).

steps.title: Extract duplicated issueJsonWithPlan helper into plan-context.ts
steps.description: Three command files contain a byte-identical copy of issueJsonWithPlan: src/commands/list.ts:155-164, src/commands/ready.ts:194-203, src/commands/search.ts:16-25. Confirmed via `diff` — bodies are identical. This is drift risk: any future change to the JSON shape (e.g. PLAN_SPEC adds plan_outcome) has to be mirrored across three files. Fix: move the function (and its return type) into src/plan-context.ts as a named export next to summarisePlanChildren / planLineSuffix (the natural home — plan-context.ts already owns plan-annotation helpers for these same call sites). Update the three command files to import it. No behavior change; existing tests for list/ready/search JSON output should continue to pass unchanged.

steps.title: Annotate sd blocked --json with plan_status/plan_children for parity with list/ready/search
steps.description: src/commands/blocked.ts emits `{ success, command: 'blocked', issues: blocked, count }` in JSON mode (lines 36-43), but unlike sd list / sd ready / sd search (which all run each issue through issueJsonWithPlan and surface plan_status / plan_children), it ships the raw Issue array with no plan annotation. seeds-92ef already established the parity contract for search; blocked is the remaining outlier consumers (warren) hit. Fix: in src/commands/blocked.ts, loadPlanContext once after readIssues, and map blocked issues through the shared issueJsonWithPlan helper (extracted in step 1 if landed, otherwise duplicate the same shape pending step 1) in the json case. Add a test in src/commands/blocked.test.ts (create it if missing — there's no existing file) covering: (a) blocked issue with no plan -> JSON has no plan_status key (b) blocked issue whose plan_id resolves -> JSON has plan_status and plan_children matching the plan row. Human-mode output (plain/compact/default) is out of scope for this step.

steps.title: Use VERSION constant from src/version.ts in sd upgrade instead of re-reading package.json
steps.description: src/commands/upgrade.ts:7-10 defines getCurrentVersion() which reads `../../package.json` via Bun.file and parses it. The project ships src/version.ts (single-line `export const VERSION = ...`) precisely so callers can read the version without CLI side-effects (see comment in src/index.ts), and CI verifies version.ts and package.json stay in lockstep. The upgrade path re-reading package.json is both a drift risk (if version.ts and package.json disagreed for any reason, upgrade would diverge from --version output) and dead weight given an in-tree import suffices. Fix: replace `getCurrentVersion()` with `import { VERSION } from '../version.ts'` and inline its uses; drop the now-unused new URL / Bun.file dance. Existing upgrade tests should continue to pass — verify by running src/commands/*.test.ts (or grep for an upgrade.test.ts; if none, no new test is required since the observable output is unchanged).

steps.title: Use brand/printSuccess/printWarning helpers in sd upgrade output
steps.description: src/commands/upgrade.ts uses chalk.green('✔') (lines 32, 52, 78) and chalk.yellow('!') (line 34) as ad-hoc colored prefixes, but the rest of the CLI standardizes on the forest palette via src/output.ts: brand('✓') for success, printWarning for warnings, printSuccess for success-with-message. Glyph also differs (✔ heavy check vs ✓ light check used everywhere else). Fix: replace the chalk.green('✔')-prefixed success lines with printSuccess('Already up to date (<ver>)') / printSuccess('Upgraded to <latest>'), and the chalk.yellow('!')-prefixed update-available line with printWarning('Update available: <current> → <latest>'). The `Upgrading <pkg> from <a> to <b>...` line (plain console.log) can stay plain — the goal is consistency on success/warning glyphs, not adding color to every line. JSON output is untouched.

steps.title: Emit JSON error on unknown-command branch in src/index.ts when --json is present
steps.description: src/index.ts:148-168 detects an unknown first positional command and writes `Unknown command: <name>[. Did you mean <best>?]` to stderr as plain text, then sets exit code 1. The rest of the CLI honors `--json` for error paths (every command threads jsonMode through and the top-level main().catch() also emits JSON when --json is in argv). The unknown-command branch is the lone exception: a caller that runs `sd nosuchcmd --json` gets a plain-text stderr line and zero stdout, breaking JSON-only consumers. Fix: in the `!knownNames.includes(firstArg)` branch, mirror the main().catch() shape — when `rawArgs.includes('--json')` (the same check used at module top for the catch handler), write `JSON.stringify({ success: false, command: firstArg, error: 'Unknown command: <name>[. Did you mean <best>?]' })` via Bun.write(Bun.stdout, ...) and still set process.exitCode = 1; otherwise keep the existing stderr behavior. Add a test (extend src/suggestions.test.ts or a new src/cli-smoke.test.ts case) asserting the JSON shape for `sd nosuchcmd --json`.

steps.title: Append missing merge=union .gitattributes lines per-file in sd init
steps.description: src/commands/init.ts:47-56 reads an existing .gitattributes and short-circuits if it includes the substring '.seeds/issues.jsonl', appending all three merge=union lines only when issues.jsonl is absent. Edge case: a repo initialized by an older seeds version (or a user who hand-edited .gitattributes to add only the issues line) will permanently miss the plans.jsonl and templates.jsonl merge=union directives, so parallel-branch merges on those files won't auto-resolve. Fix: split the entry into three lines and, for each, check independently whether it's present (e.g. `existing.includes('.seeds/plans.jsonl')`); append only the missing ones, preserving a single trailing newline. Add a test in src/commands/init.test.ts that pre-creates `.gitattributes` containing only the issues.jsonl line and asserts `sd init` (when .seeds/ is also missing or being re-initialized) appends the plans.jsonl and templates.jsonl lines. Keep the existing fresh-init behavior (no .gitattributes -> write all three) byte-identical.

sections.context: The seeds repo (this repo — @os-eco/seeds-cli, the `sd` CLI) is currently below Agent-Readiness Level 5. Warren (the os-eco flagship) and canopy (M1) are already at L5; the rest of os-eco — plot, mulch, burrow, and now seeds — needs to match warren's ceiling. Seeds is the cross-repo issue-tracking surface every other os-eco tool depends on, so closing this uplift unlocks ecosystem-wide L5. The 7 children below port the L5 toolkit (templates/l5-toolkit/ at the os-eco root) into seeds, baselining ratchet budgets from seeds' current state, then seal the milestone with a passing `check:all` and an interactive `/readiness-report` run from seeds/.
sections.approach: Sequentially apply 7 implementer steps, each closing a discrete band of VAL-SEEDS-* assertions from /Users/jayminwest/Projects/os-eco/docs/l5-uplift/validation-contract.md. Step 1 fills the governance baseline catch-up (dependabot/cooldown, PR template, issue templates). Step 2 ports the configs + .devcontainer + .env.example + .gitignore extension. Step 3 adds the file-size and debt-marker ratchets with seeds-specific tracker prefixes. Step 4 wires coverage + reporters + junit. Step 5 authors AGENTS.md, the validator, RUNBOOK.md, and the first .factory/skills/<name>/SKILL.md. Step 6 adds pino + pino-pretty with redact, the pre-commit hook + prepare script, and the labels.yml + sync workflow. Step 7 wires check:all into both package.json and ci.yml, runs the four-gate suite, and prompts the user to run /readiness-report. Each child uses adapt-don't-overwrite semantics: existing strengths (the large CLAUDE.md, any existing dependabot.yml that already has the cooldown block) are preserved or extended, not replaced.

sections.context: Code-patrol scan of seeds @ 2026-05-29 surfaced two production-affecting consistency/quality issues: (1) the top-level error handler in src/index.ts dynamically imports ./log.ts, which loads pino — but pino lives in devDependencies, so end-user installs of @os-eco/seeds-cli will throw 'Cannot find module pino' instead of reporting the actual command error; (2) the --json output contract is inconsistent: outputJson() emits 2-space-indented JSON, but three exit points in src/index.ts emit compact JSON for the same --json flag, so the shape callers see depends on which code path fires.
sections.behavior_invariant: All existing tests in bun test continue to pass. The `sd` CLI exit codes, command surface, public API signatures, and stdout/stderr JSON keys remain unchanged. Logging behavior under SEEDS_DEBUG=1 (when pino is installed) is preserved. --json callers continue to receive valid JSON on a single line break; the only observable change is uniform indentation across all --json exit points and graceful handling when pino is absent.
sections.approach: Two narrow, independent fixes followed by a release. Neither touches public APIs, command surfaces, or dependencies. Step 1 hardens the lazy pino import in the main() catch so a missing pino module no longer masks the real error. Step 2 normalizes --json formatting across the four exit points in src/index.ts and src/output.ts so the same flag emits the same shape. Step 3 releases.

steps.title: Make lazy pino import in main() catch resilient to missing module
steps.description:
src/index.ts:187-199 wires `await import('./log.ts')` inside the global error handler. ./log.ts imports pino at module top, and pino is declared in devDependencies (package.json), not dependencies. For any non-dev install of @os-eco/seeds-cli, every thrown error from a command will trigger a 'Cannot find module pino' rejection that surfaces *instead of* the actual error message the user needs.

Fix: wrap the dynamic import + log.debug call in a try/catch so a missing pino is silently swallowed (debug observability is best-effort and only matters under SEEDS_DEBUG=1, which dev-style installs use). Do NOT move pino to dependencies — that violates the no-dependency-changes rule and AGENTS.md's 'minimal runtime dependencies' contract. Do NOT change the user-facing error message routing (stderr chalk red or JSON payload) — leave that exactly as-is.

Add a test in src/log.test.ts (or a sibling) verifying that when ./log.ts import throws synthetically (e.g. via a mock), the main catch still emits the user-facing error on stderr/stdout and sets exitCode=1. A minimal regression test is enough; no need to actually uninstall pino.

Files: src/index.ts (the main().catch block, ~lines 187-205). Tests: src/index.test.ts or extend src/log.test.ts. Acceptance: `bun test`, `bun run lint`, `bun run typecheck` all green; manual sanity that a real error still reaches stderr.

steps.title: Normalize --json indentation across all CLI exit points
steps.description:
The --json output contract is split between two styles:
  - outputJson() in src/output.ts:17 emits `JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)` (2-space indent).
  - Three exit points in src/index.ts emit compact `JSON.stringify(payload)` for the same --json flag:
      * src/index.ts:140  --version --json
      * src/index.ts:173  unknown-command path
      * src/index.ts:198  main().catch error envelope

Callers piping `sd ... --json` get different shapes depending on which path fires, which complicates parsing and snapshot tests.

Fix: pick ONE style and apply it everywhere. Recommended: keep outputJson()'s 2-space-indented form (current dominant style — confirmed by every src/commands/*.ts call site). Update the three src/index.ts emissions to either (a) route through outputJson() if convenient, or (b) inline `JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2)`. Trailing newline must be preserved at each site (currently all four append `\n`).

Update any tests that assert on the exact JSON form of --version --json, unknown-command --json, or the main-catch error envelope. Search: `grep -rn 'unknown command\|did you mean\|Cannot find\|--version --json' src --include='*.test.ts'`.

No behavior change beyond whitespace. Do NOT alter JSON keys, key order, or success/error envelope shape. Do NOT change which stream (stdout vs stderr) each path uses.

Files: src/index.ts (lines ~138-144, ~166-175, ~187-202). Tests: update assertions in src/cli-smoke.test.ts and src/suggestions.test.ts if they pin compact-JSON layout. Acceptance: `bun test`, `bun run lint`, `bun run typecheck` green; manually run `bun src/index.ts --version --json` and `bun src/index.ts bogusCommand --json` and confirm both are indented like every other --json response.

sections.context: Automated nightwatch patrol on 2026-05-30 surfaced four small quality issues in the seeds repo: (1) a real bug where `sd update --title` stores the user's title without trimming whitespace, while `sd create --title` already trims — same field, two storage shapes; (2) a JSON/non-JSON behavior mismatch in `sd upgrade --check` where the non-JSON path sets `process.exitCode = 1` when an update is available but the --json path does not; (3) dead code — `bunx knip` flags three unused exports (`writeConfig`, `isFormatMode`, `formatExtensionsLine`) and four unused exported types (`DomainSource`, `SectionRequest`, `PlanOutcome`, `ConvoyStatus`) that no caller imports; (4) configuration drift — `knip.json` emits 7 hints about stale ignore entries (dist/**, coverage/**, test-results/**, .seeds/**), no-match `extensions/**/*.ts` patterns, and a redundant `src/index.ts` entry. None of these are blocking but each is a small, isolated, in-scope fix. Lint, typecheck, all 919 tests, and quality-gate scripts are currently green; these changes must keep them green.

No public TypeScript signature changes, no new dependencies, no new CLI flags or subcommands.
sections.approach: Land each finding as a single small PR. Each step touches a narrow file set, ships with the new/updated tests that lock in the fix, and runs the full gate suite locally before close. No abstractions introduced, no module boundaries moved. If during dead-code removal any export turns out to actually be referenced from outside the project (extensions, scripts), keep it and note it in the close reason instead of forcing the removal.

sections.context: Routine code-patrol pass over the seeds repo. All quality gates (`bun test`, `bun run lint`, `bun run typecheck`) currently pass on main, but a scan of the CLI command surface, JSON-output contract, store layer, and test coverage turned up six concrete findings: 2 JSON-envelope inconsistencies, 1 missing input-validation bug, 1 unnecessary `as any` cluster, 1 test-coverage gap across five commands, and 1 file-lock race condition. None require API changes, dependency changes, or new commands — they are internal fixes only.
sections.behavior_invariant: After every step, the full quality-gate set must still pass: `bun run lint`, `bun run typecheck`, `bun test`, `bun run check:debt`, `bun run check:size`, `bun run check:coverage`, `bun run check:agents`. No public CLI surface change (no new commands, no removed flags, no removed JSON keys). All existing `--json` keys keep their names and types — the envelope fixes are purely *additive* (adding `success`/`command` alongside the existing payload key). The JSONL on-disk schema is unchanged. `sd plan submit` continues to spawn the same children for the same plan input. The advisory-lock contract (one writer, atomic temp-file rename) remains.
sections.approach:
One small PR per finding, ordered so envelope/validation/type/test fixes (independent, low risk) land first and the lock-race correctness fix lands after the test scaffolding in step 5 makes regression testing easier. Final step is the standard release bundle.

Step-by-step targets:

1. `src/commands/plan.ts:404-407` — `if (jsonMode) { await outputJson({ plan_request: planRequest }); return; }` is the only `outputJson` call in the CLI that omits the `{success, command}` envelope. Change to `outputJson({ success: true, command: "plan prompt", plan_request: planRequest })`. Update plan.test.ts plan-prompt JSON assertions and any docs.

2. `src/index.ts:135-148` — the `--version --json` early-exit emits `{ name, version, runtime, platform }` via `console.log(JSON.stringify(...))`. Wrap in `{ success: true, command: "version", name, version, runtime, platform }` and emit through `Bun.write(Bun.stdout, ...)` so the output path matches `outputJson`. Update cli-smoke.test.ts or add a small test for the new envelope.

3. `src/commands/tpl.ts:97-101` — `tpl step add` reads `flags.priority` (default "2"), runs `Number.parseInt(priorityStr, 10)`, and stores the result unchecked. `Number.parseInt("abc")` returns `NaN` and large numbers are accepted. Mirror the validation already in `src/commands/create.ts:62-65` and `src/commands/update.ts:122-125`: support `P0..P4` parsing AND range-check `0..4`, throw `--priority must be 0-4 or P0-P4`. Add tpl.test.ts cases for the rejection paths.

4. `src/test-harness.ts:154,166,208` — three `(Bun as any).write` casts in the Bun.write monkey-patch. Replace with a typed local interface or a single `Bun.write`-typed alias so `as any` disappears. `bun run typecheck` must stay clean and `bun test src/test-harness.test.ts` must still pass.

5. Co-located unit tests are missing for `src/commands/blocked.ts`, `src/commands/stats.ts`, `src/commands/migrate.ts`, `src/commands/close.ts`, and `src/commands/plan-show.ts`. Add `blocked.test.ts`, `stats.test.ts`, `migrate.test.ts`, `close.test.ts`, and `plan-show.test.ts` using the existing in-process test harness pattern (`src/test-harness.ts`). Each file needs at least one happy-path case and one error-path case (e.g. close non-existent id, stats on empty store, plan-show unknown plan id, migrate on non-beads dir, blocked with mixed open/closed blockers). `bun run check:coverage` must not regress and the new tests must pass `bun run check:agents`.

6. `src/store.ts:18-43` `acquireLock` — when the lock file is detected as stale via `statSync` + mtime check, the code calls `unlinkSync(lock); continue;`. Between two concurrent processes both seeing a stale lock, process A's `unlinkSync` can remove process B's *freshly acquired* (non-stale) lock if B's openSync(wx) happens to win the race between A's stat and A's unlink. Fix by making the stale-claim atomic: e.g. rename the stale lock to a uniquely-named path before unlinking, and only proceed when the rename succeeds; or check the lock's mtime again after unlink and before the next openSync. Add a regression test in store.test.ts that simulates the concurrent-stale scenario.

7. Release: run `/release` per `.claude/commands/release.md` — bumps version in lockstep via `bun run version:bump`, adds a CHANGELOG entry summarising the patrol, runs final gates, and produces a tag-ready commit.
